question: what adjustmets help me shoot slower?
content:
WHEN YOU WANT TO BOWL WITH LESS SPEED, YOU NEED TO MAKE SPECIFIC ADJUSTMENTS TO YOUR SETUP AND MOVEMENT SO YOUR TIMING STAYS CONSISTENT, BUT THE BALL TRAVELS SLOWER DOWN THE LANE. HERE’S HOW TO DO IT:
1.  MOVE CLOSER TO THE FOUL LINE
STAND ABOUT ONE AND A HALF FEET CLOSER TO THE FOUL LINE THAN YOU NORMALLY WOULD. THIS SHORTENS YOUR APPROACH, SO YOU TAKE SMALLER STEPS BEFORE RELEASING THE BALL.
2. LOWER YOUR BALL POSITION
INSTEAD OF HOLDING THE BALL WITH YOUR ELBOW BENT AT A 90-DEGREE ANGLE (LIKE AN “L” SHAPE), LET YOUR ELBOW OPEN UP TO ABOUT 120 DEGREES. THIS MEANS THE BALL STARTS LOWER, CLOSER TO YOUR WAIST THAN YOUR CHEST.
WHY THESE CHANGES WORK:
BY MOVING CLOSER, YOU HAVE LESS DISTANCE TO BUILD UP SPEED WITH YOUR LEGS.
BY STARTING WITH THE BALL LOWER, YOUR BACKSWING IS SHORTER, SO THE BALL DOESN’T GAIN AS MUCH MOMENTUM.
BOTH CHANGES TOGETHER MEAN YOU CAN KEEP YOUR TIMING AND RHYTHM THE SAME, BUT THE BALL WILL NATURALLY TRAVEL SLOWER BECAUSE IT HAS LESS ENERGY FROM BOTH YOUR APPROACH AND YOUR SWING.
